ZealPHP Framework — Complete Reference
=======================================

ZealPHP is an async PHP framework built on OpenSwoole. One server handles HTTP, WebSocket, SSE, task workers, and sessions. It's the PHP runtime for AI web apps.

## Installation

    composer create-project sibidharan/zealphp-project:^0.1.1 my-app
    cd my-app && php app.php

Requires PHP 8.3+, OpenSwoole extension, uopz extension.
System deps: sudo bash setup.sh

## Routing

Flask-style routes with reflection-based parameter injection:

    $app->route('/user/{id}', function($id) {
        return ['id' => $id, 'name' => 'Alice'];
    });

    // Multiple methods
    $app->route('/data', ['methods' => ['GET','POST']], function($request) {
        return $request->get['q'] ?? 'no query';
    });

Parameter injection by name — $request, $response, $app, {param} names, or defaults:

    $app->route('/page/{slug}', function($slug, $request, $response, $title = 'Default') {
        // $slug from URL, $request/$response injected, $title uses default
    });

Route types:
- $app->route('/path', $handler) — exact match
- $app->nsRoute('admin', '/dashboard', $handler) — namespaced: /admin/dashboard
- $app->nsPathRoute('api', $handler) — catch-all: /api/* delegates to ZealAPI
- $app->patternRoute('#^/files/(.+)$#', $handler) — regex

## Return Values

    return 404;              // HTTP status code
    return ['key' => 'val']; // JSON (auto Content-Type)
    return 'Hello';          // HTML string
    return $generator;       // SSR streaming

    // void + echo = output buffer captured
    echo '<h1>Hello</h1>';

## SSR Streaming (4 patterns)

1. Generator yield — each yield sent immediately:

    $app->route('/stream', function() {
        return (function() {
            yield '<html><body>';
            yield '<h1>Loading...</h1>';
            co::sleep(1);
            yield '<p>Done!</p>';
            yield '</body></html>';
        })();
    });

2. App::renderStream() — streaming templates with param injection:

    // template/users/list.php
    <?php return function($users) {
        yield '<ul>';
        foreach ($users as $u) yield "<li>{$u->name}</li>";
        yield '</ul>';
    };

    // Route — compose streams
    $app->route('/users', fn() => (function() {
        yield from App::renderStream('header', ['title' => 'Users']);
        yield from App::renderStream('users/list', ['users' => $allUsers]);
        yield from App::renderStream('footer');
    })());

3. $response->stream($fn) — fine-grained control:

    $app->route('/download', function($response) {
        $response->stream(function($write) {
            $write('chunk 1');
            co::sleep(1);
            $write('chunk 2');
        });
    });

4. $response->sse($fn) — Server-Sent Events:

    $app->route('/events', function($response) {
        $response->sse(function($emit) {
            for ($i = 1; $i <= 5; $i++) {
                co::sleep(1);
                $emit(json_encode(['tick' => $i]), 'update');
            }
            $emit(json_encode(['done' => true]), 'complete');
        });
    });

## Templates

    App::render('page', ['title' => 'Hello']);           // echoes
    $html = App::renderToString('page', ['title' => 'Hello']); // returns string
    $gen = App::renderStream('page', ['title' => 'Hello']);     // returns Generator

Template files in template/ directory. _master.php is the layout.
Streaming templates return a Closure: return function($var) { yield ...; };

## WebSocket

    $app->ws('/chat',
        function($server, $frame) {           // onMessage
            $server->push($frame->fd, 'Echo: ' . $frame->data);
        },
        function($server, $request) {         // onOpen
            $server->push($request->fd, 'Welcome!');
        },
        function($server, $fd) {              // onClose
            echo "Client {$fd} left\n";
        }
    );

Auto-drops PING/PONG/CONTINUATION frames. Sends CLOSE 1001 on shutdown.

## Store (Cross-Worker Shared Memory)

    // Before $app->run() — shared on fork
    Store::make('sessions', 1024, [
        'user'  => [\OpenSwoole\Table::TYPE_STRING, 64],
        'count' => [\OpenSwoole\Table::TYPE_INT, 4],
    ]);

    // In any worker
    Store::set('sessions', 'key123', ['user' => 'Alice', 'count' => 1]);
    $row = Store::get('sessions', 'key123');
    Store::incr('sessions', 'key123', 'count', 1);
    Store::del('sessions', 'key123');
    Store::exists('sessions', 'key123');
    $total = Store::count('sessions');

## Counter (Lock-Free Atomic Integer)

    Counter::make('visitors');
    Counter::increment('visitors');
    Counter::decrement('visitors');
    $val = Counter::get('visitors');
    Counter::compareAndSet('visitors', $expected, $new);

## Coroutines

    use function OpenSwoole\Core\Coroutine\run;
    use OpenSwoole\Coroutine as co;
    use OpenSwoole\Coroutine\Channel;

    // Parallel execution
    $app->route('/parallel', function() {
        $ch = new Channel(2);
        go(function() use ($ch) {
            co::sleep(1);
            $ch->push(['api1' => 'result']);
        });
        go(function() use ($ch) {
            co::sleep(1);
            $ch->push(['api2' => 'result']);
        });
        return ['r1' => $ch->pop(), 'r2' => $ch->pop()]; // ~1s total, not 2s
    });

HOOK_ALL makes MySQL, Redis, file I/O, curl all async automatically.

## Middleware (PSR-15)

    use ZealPHP\Middleware\CorsMiddleware;
    use ZealPHP\Middleware\ETagMiddleware;

    $app->addMiddleware(new CorsMiddleware(['*'], ['GET','POST','OPTIONS']));
    $app->addMiddleware(new ETagMiddleware());

Last-added runs first (outermost). ResponseMiddleware always innermost.

Built-in:
- CorsMiddleware — CORS preflight + Access-Control-* headers
- ETagMiddleware — W/"md5" ETag + 304 Not Modified
- CompressionMiddleware — gzip/deflate (only if OpenSwoole http_compression disabled)

## Sessions

    session_start();
    $_SESSION['user'] = 'Alice';    // works in superglobals mode
    $g->session['user'] = 'Alice';  // works in coroutine mode

File-backed, coroutine-safe. session_*() functions overridden via uopz.

## ZealAPI (File-Based REST)

    // api/users/get.php — becomes GET /api/users
    $get = function() {
        return ['users' => [...]];
    };

    // api/users/post.php — becomes POST /api/users
    $post = function() {
        $body = json_decode(file_get_contents('php://input'), true);
        return ['created' => true];
    };

Variable name must match basename. Auto-bound as closure with $this = ZealAPI instance.

## Timers

    // Inside onWorkerStart or request handler
    App::tick(5000, function() {
        echo "Every 5 seconds\n";
    });

    App::after(10000, function() {
        echo "Once after 10 seconds\n";
    });

## Task Workers

    // task/backup.php
    App::getServer()->task(['handler' => '/task/backup', 'args' => [...]]);

## HTTP Features

    $response->redirect('/new-url', 301);    // 301, 302, 307, 308
    header('X-Custom: value');               // works via uopz override
    setcookie('name', 'value', 0, '/', '', false, true, 'Lax');
    http_response_code(201);

HEAD auto-mapped to GET (body stripped). OPTIONS returns 204 + Allow header.
OpenSwoole http_compression enabled by default (gzip).

## Legacy App Support

    App::superglobals(true);   // enables $_GET, $_POST, $_SESSION
    App::setFallback(function() {
        include 'wordpress/index.php';  // catch-all for unmatched routes
    });

CGI worker runs PHP files in separate process for true global scope isolation.
WordPress, Drupal, any PHP app runs unmodified.

## CLI

    php app.php start -p 8080 -d    # daemonized
    php app.php stop                # stop via PID file
    php app.php status              # check running

Env vars: ZEALPHP_WORKERS=16, ZEALPHP_TASK_WORKERS=4

## Performance

67,000 req/s on 4 workers (local quad-core). 21ms p90 latency. 0 failures.
Multi-process workers + coroutines for true parallelism.
Shared memory via Store — no Redis needed for simple state.

## G Class (Per-Request State)

    $g = G::instance();
    $g->get          // query params
    $g->post         // POST body
    $g->cookie       // cookies
    $g->session      // session data
    $g->server       // server vars (REMOTE_ADDR, etc.)
    $g->files        // uploaded files
    $g->zealphp_request   // ZealPHP\HTTP\Request
    $g->zealphp_response  // ZealPHP\HTTP\Response

Coroutine mode (recommended): per-coroutine isolation via Coroutine::getContext().
Superglobals mode: process-wide singleton proxying to $GLOBALS.

## IOStreamWrapper

php://input reads from $g->zealphp_request->parent->getContent() instead of PHP's built-in.
Registered once per worker. Other php:// streams delegated to original wrapper.

## Key Architecture Points

- OpenSwoole WebSocket\Server (extends HTTP\Server — all HTTP routes work)
- uopz overrides: header(), setcookie(), session_start(), etc. redirected to per-request objects
- Route reflection cached at registration — zero per-request overhead
- Middleware stack reversed: last-added = outermost
- Generators work everywhere: routes, public files, API handlers, templates
